# FilesHub

> FilesHub is a zero-cost file-storage and developer-utility API. You upload a file
> over plain HTTP to `POST /api/v1/objects` with an `X-API-Key` header and get back a
> stable public URL plus a ULID. You can list, download, and delete objects, set each
> file's visibility (`public` or `private`), and optionally auto-expire files. Beyond
> storage it exposes 50+ utility endpoints (hashing, encoding, converters, QR codes,
> short URLs, OTP, an encryption vault, image/PDF processing). It is the upload backend
> used by every Aoneahsan/Zaions project. Built and maintained by Ahsan Mahmood.

- Product: https://fileshub.zaions.com
- Docs: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com
- API base URL: https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/v1
- Auth: `X-API-Key: <your project key>` header on every request
- License: MIT (docs) · Author: Ahsan Mahmood (https://aoneahsan.com)

## Core concepts

- Authentication: every request carries an `X-API-Key` header. Keys are per project,
  with `read` and `write` permissions and optional origin / `X-App-Id` restrictions.
- Object: an uploaded file. Identified by a ULID `public_id`. Has a `mime_type`,
  `size_bytes`, a `visibility` (`public` or `private`), an optional `expires_at`, and a
  `url` you can link to or download.
- Visibility: `public` objects are downloadable by anyone at their URL; `private` objects
  require a `read`-permission key from the same project. The integration default across
  Aoneahsan/Zaions apps is `visibility: public`.

## Object API (the part every app integrates)

- Upload: `POST /api/v1/objects` — multipart form field `file` (required), optional
  `visibility` (`public`|`private`), optional `expires_in_days` or `expires_at`. Returns
  `201` with `{ public_id, project_id, visibility, mime_type, size_bytes, url, expires_at }`.
- Download / view: `GET /api/v1/objects/{public_id}` — streams the file. No key needed for
  public objects; a `read` key is required for private ones.
- List: `GET /api/v1/objects` — paginated (`page`, `per_page` max 100, optional `visibility`
  filter). Returns `{ data: [...], meta: { current_page, per_page, total } }`.
- Delete: `DELETE /api/v1/objects/{public_id}` — removes the database record and the stored
  file. Returns `200`.

- API overview: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/api/overview
- Upload an object: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/api/upload-object
- Get / download an object: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/api/get-object
- List objects: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/api/list-objects
- Delete an object: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/api/delete-object
- Errors & limits: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/api/errors-and-limits

- Browser & mobile uploads: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/guides/browser-and-mobile-uploads
- Platform services (50+ utilities): https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/platform-services
- FAQ: https://docs.fileshub.zaions.com/faq

## Honest limits

- FilesHub is currently a single-region, locally-stored backend (not a multi-region CDN).
  Keep large media on a CDN if you need global edge delivery; FilesHub is built for app
  assets, user uploads, exports, and share links.
- Default max upload size is 10 MB (configurable per deployment via `FILESHUB_MAX_UPLOAD_MB`).
- There is no public client SDK package yet — you integrate over plain HTTP (curl / fetch /
  axios / any language). A TypeScript SDK is archived pending API stabilisation.
